## Religious change at a societial level indicates "clocklike" decline

```{r  poprelchange, fig.cap= "Clock-like Decline in Proportion of Christian Affiliation since 1966 at about 1%-2% per year", cache = TRUE, code_folding=TRUE, include = FALSE}
# Show decline in religion at the Society Level, using census data
# religious decline in new Zealand

# import census data
declineUSE <-
  as.data.frame(read.csv(
    here::here("data", "declineUSE.csv")
    ))
# select only religious people

decline <- declineUSE %>%
  select(-"NoReligion") 

# decline  NZ census counts for Christians as a percentage of the total population


library(ggplot2)

pr <- ggplot(decline, aes(y = PercentagePopulation, x = Years)) + theme_bw() +
  geom_smooth(method = "glm") +
  geom_point(
    stat = "identity",
    colour = "red",
    fill = "white",
    size = 2,
    stroke = 1
  ) + labs(x = "Time", y = "Percentage Religious") +
  labs(title = "New ZealandCensus; proportional decline in religious affiliation: years 1966-2018")  +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_bw()
pr

```

Figure \@ref(fig:hmmchange) a. presents New Zealand Census
data for society-level religious affiliation. The census
data show a clock-like decline in religious affiliation at
the level of the New Zealand population since 1966 of about
1% loss in religious affiliation per year, and more
recently, closer to 2% loss. This suggests that people are
becoming less religious over time.

## Religious change at the individual level consists of opposing processes

However, society-level change is the average of two opposing
processes that occur at the level of individuals:

-   **religious affiliation**: people might affiliate from a
    position of no-affiliation.
-   **religious disaffiliation**: people might disaffiliate
    from a position of affiliation.

These two opposing processes occur against a background of
stability - most people remain constant in their religious
affiliation or disaffiliation.

Here, we use a Hidden Markov Model to estimate rates of
religious affiliation and disaffiliation across the New
Zealand population from 2009/10 -- 2020 in a sample of
respondents who completed at least two NZAVS waves ($N$ =
48272).

We find that, annually, the society-level rate of religious
disaffiliation in New Zealand is .02%. That is the
difference between the disaffiliation rate (Pr = .03) and
the religious affiliation rate (Pr = .01). NZAVS data do not
capture fluctuations from migration, hence, the NZAVS
closely approximates the national census estimate for net
religious change at the society level.

```{r  msm, cache = TRUE, include = TRUE}
# Using markov models, show that change within people as they age differs from the society-wide trend
df <- readRDS(here::here("data", "df"))

length(unique(df$Id))


#create dataframe
dat  <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1) %>% # filtering only people who have responded in a year
  dplyr::select(Wave, Age, Wave, Religious1 , Id, Relid, years) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(yearW  = as.numeric(Wave) - 1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>%
  filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  add_tally(Religious1) %>%  # get at least 2 measures
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Religious1)) %>%
  ungroup() %>% # get at least 2 measures
  arrange(Id, yearW) %>% # get at least 2 measures
  dplyr::filter(n>1) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  group_by(Id) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n() != 0) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n() != 1) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n>1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(Id, yearW)
#nrow(df)
head(dat)

sum(dat$n < 2)
# unique ids
length(unique(dat$Id)) #53990

table(dat$n)
sum(is.na(dat$Religious1))
#table1::table1( ~Wave, data = dat)

```

Participants by waves

```{r}
count_waves_participants <- function(x){
  out<-dplyr::count(tally(group_by(x, Id), sort = TRUE, name="number_waves"), number_waves)
  print(out)
}

count_waves_participants(dat)


```

MSM Analysis

```{r  msm2, cache = TRUE, eval = FALSE}
### MSM ANALYIS BELOW ###
# commenting out model to save time (which I've saved in a file, below)
# initial matrix for transitions
library("msm")

Q0 <- rbind(c(.9, .1),
            c(.1, .9))

# intensity matrix
Q0

# Initialise
crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(Religious1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = dat,
                      qmatrix = Q0)

# model
m1  <- msm(
  Religious1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = dat,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
  ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE,
  # Not running without this assumption.
  gen.inits = TRUE
)

m1
saveRDS(m1, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1.rds" ))
```

Transition probabilities (averaged)

```{r}
# read model, (saved to save time)
m1 <-readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1.rds" ))
m1
#probability matrix
dt<-  print( round( msm::pmatrix.msm( m1 ) , 2 ) )

# label cols, rows:
rownames(dt) = c("Not-religious time 0", "Religious time 0")
colnames(dt) = c("Not-religious t+1","Religious t+1")

#table
dt%>%
 kbl(caption = "Hidden Markov Model: inferred population average religious state change, years 2009-2020")%>%
  kable_material(c("hover"))
```

LaTeX table

```{r, eval = FALSE}
dt %>%
  kbl(booktabs = T,
      format = "latex",
      caption = "Hidden Markov Model: inferred population average religious state change, years 2009-2020")
  
```

We can compare this with the raw (un-modelled) state
transition, which overstates overall change.

```{r}
# example change in wave 2018/ wave 2019
min(dat$yearW)
dat2 <- dat %>%
  filter(yearW == 10 | yearW == 11)
tail(dat2)

raw <-msm::statetable.msm(Religious1, Id, data = dat2)
# calculates proportions by rows
out<- round( prop.table(raw, 1), 2)

rownames(out) = c("Not-religious time 0", "Religious time 0")
colnames(out) = c("Not-religious t+1","Religious t+1")

out %>%
 kbl(caption = "Raw sample religious state change: years 2009-2020") %>%
  kable_material(c("hover"))
```

LaTeX table

```{r, eval = FALSE}
out %>%
  kbl(booktabs = T,
      format = "latex",
      caption ="Raw sample religious state change: years 2009-2020")
  
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# latex
xtable(out)
```

```{r  hmmchange, fig.cap="Hidden Markov Model reveals religious change within people differs from the society wide-aggregate trend. As people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.", echo =FALSE}
# Graph
oz<- as.matrix( msm::pmatrix.msm( m1 ) )
oz <- round(oz, digits = 2)


stateNames <- c("Secular (.64)","Religious (.36)")
row.names(oz) <- stateNames; colnames(oz) <- stateNames
oz
## need to transpose or else arrows go in the wrong direction
toz <- t(oz) 

#library(pracma)
plotmat(toz,
       # pos = c(1,2), 
       # lwd = 1, 
       # box.lwd = 2, 
        cex.txt = 0.9, 
        box.size = 0.09, 
       # box.type = "circle", 
        box.prop = 0.5,
        arr.pos =  0.4, 
        shadow.size = 0.001,
        box.col = c("lightgreen","lightblue","orange"),
       # arr.length=.1,
       # arr.width=.1,
        self.cex = .4,
        self.shifty = -.01,
        self.shiftx = .12,
        main = "Hidden Markov Model reveals religious switching\nNew Zealand: N = 53990, years 2009-2021")

```

## Graph

```{r  hmmchange2, cache = TRUE, fig.cap="Hidden Markov Model reveals religious change within people differs from the society wide-aggregate trend. As people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from much greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.", include = TRUE, code_folding=TRUE}

# note to self, this code can be improved
### CREATE PLOT  ###  CONVERSION AGE PLOT
## 20 year olds

#m1 <-readRDS(here::here( "models","mnm-simpson-paradox-religious-change" ))

p20 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 20), ci = "normal")
p20.Convert <- p20$estimates[1, 2]
p20.Convert.L <- p20$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p20.Convert.U <- p20$U[1, 2]
con.20 <- c(20, p20.Convert, p20.Convert.L, p20.Convert.U)
## 30 year olds
p30 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 30), ci = "normal")
p30.Convert <- p30$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p30.Convert.L <- p30$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p30.Convert.U <- p30$U[1, 2]
#combine
con.30 <- c(30, p30.Convert, p30.Convert.L, p30.Convert.U)
## 40 year old
p40 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 40), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p40.Convert <- p40$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p40.Convert.L <- p40$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p40.Convert.U <- p40$U[1, 2]
con.40 <- c(40, p40.Convert, p40.Convert.L, p40.Convert.U)
## 50 year old
p50 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 50), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p50.Convert <- p50$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p50.Convert.L <- p50$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p50.Convert.U <- p50$U[1, 2]
con.50 <- c(50, p50.Convert, p50.Convert.L, p50.Convert.U)
## 60 year olds
p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p60.Convert <- p60$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p60.Convert.L <- p60$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p60.Convert.U <- p60$U[1, 2]
con.60 <- c(60, p60.Convert, p60.Convert.L, p60.Convert.U)
## 70 year olds
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p70.Convert <- p70$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p70.Convert.L <- p70$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p70.Convert.U <- p70$U[1, 2]
con.70 <- c(70, p70.Convert, p70.Convert.L, p70.Convert.U)
### ## 80 year olds
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p80.Convert <- p80$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p80.Convert.L <- p80$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p80.Convert.U <- p80$U[1, 2]
con.80 <- c(80, p80.Convert, p80.Convert.L, p80.Convert.U)
# ### ## 90 year olds
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
# # Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p90.Convert <- p90$estimates[1, 2]
# # set lower bound
p90.Convert.L <- p90$L[1, 2]
p90.Convert.U <- p90$U[1, 2]
con.90 <- c(90, p90.Convert, p90.Convert.L, p90.Convert.U)
# ### create data frame for conversion plot
conplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(con.20, con.30, con.40, con.50, con.60, con.70, con.80, con.90))
colnames(conplot) <-
  c("age", "conv_probability", "conv_lower", "conv_upper")
library(ggplot2)
#library(wesanderson)
#wes_palette("Zissou1", type = c("discrete"))
#library("ggsci")

plot.con <-
  ggplot(aes(
    x = age,
    y = conv_probability,
    ymin = conv_lower,
    ymax = conv_upper
  ),
  data = conplot) + geom_point(stroke = 2, colour = "#d1495b")  +
  geom_linerange(alpha =   1, colour = "#d1495b") +
  geom_smooth(colour = "#d1495b",
              method = "loess",
              se = FALSE)  +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .1)) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Religious Affiliation Transition Probability w/ 95% CI") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Religious (Dis)affiliation Dynamics in New Zealand: years 2009-2020")

p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
p60.decon <- p60$estimates[2, 1]
p60.decon.L <- p60$L[2, 1]
p60.decon.U <- p60$U[2, 1]
decon.60 <- c(60, p60.decon, p60.decon.L, p60.decon.U)
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
p70.decon <- p70$estimates[2, 1]
p70.decon.L <- p70$L[2, 1]
p70.decon.U <- p70$U[2, 1]
decon.70 <- c(70, p70.decon, p70.decon.L, p70.decon.U)
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
p80.decon <- p80$estimates[2, 1]
p80.decon.L <- p80$L[2, 1]
p80.decon.U <- p80$U[2, 1]
decon.80 <- c(80, p80.decon, p80.decon.L, p80.decon.U)
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
p90.decon <- p90$estimates[2, 1]
p90.decon.L <- p90$L[2, 1]
p90.decon.U <- p90$U[2, 1]
decon.90 <- c(90, p90.decon, p90.decon.L, p90.decon.U)

### create data frame for conversion plot
deconplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(
    decon.20,
    decon.30,
    decon.40,
    decon.50,
    decon.60,
    decon.70,
    decon.80,
    decon.90
  ))
colnames(deconplot) <-
  c("age", "decon_probability", "decon_lower", "decon_upper")
library(ggplot2)
plot.decon <-
  ggplot(aes(
    x = age,
    y = decon_probability,
    ymin = decon_lower,
    ymax = decon_upper
  ),
  data = deconplot) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1,
             colour = "#00798c",
             stroke = 2) +   geom_linerange(alpha = 1, colour = "#00798c") +
  #scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,.1))    +
  geom_smooth(colour = "#00798c", se = T) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Religious Disaffiliation Transition Probability w/ 95% CI") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Religious (Dis)affiliation Dynamics")  +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

library(gridExtra)

#grid.arrange(plot.con,plot.decon, ncol=2)
### SUPERIMPOSE
deconplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Disaffiliation", nrow(deconplot)))
conplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Affiliation", nrow(conplot)))
deconplot2 <- deconplot %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    conv_probability = decon_probability,
    conv_lower = decon_lower,
    conv_upper = decon_upper
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-c(decon_probability, decon_upper, decon_lower))

comboplot <- rbind(conplot, deconplot2)

```{r eavl = F}
#save graph 

ggsave(
  relhmm,
  path = here::here(here::here("_posts", "religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods")),
  width = 16,
  height = 9,
  units = "in",
  filename = "relHMM.jpg",
  device = 'jpeg',
  limitsize = FALSE,
  dpi = 1200
)
```

```{r eavl = F}
#save graph 
ggsave(
  pr,
  path = here::here(here::here("_posts", "religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods")),
  width = 8,
  height = 4.5,
  units = "in",
  filename = "proprel.jpg",
  device = 'jpeg',
  limitsize = FALSE,
  dpi = 1200
)
```

However, as evident in Figure \@ref(fig:hmmchange),
individual-level NZAVS data reveal that as people age, the
religious affiliation rate gradually increases. Likewise, as
people age, the religious disaffiliation rate gradually
decreases. The age at which religious affiliation exceeds
disaffiliation is about 70 years old. The society-level
trend and individual-level trends run in the opposite
directions because there is higher relative religious
disaffiliation among young people. This example illustrates
what is called a "Simpson's Paradox." Wherever there is
potential for switching between states at an individual
level, there is on opportunity for a Simpson's Paradox. Most
facts of interest about the role of religion in a persons
life are subject to switching.

## Religious change is a Simpson's Paradox

```{r hmmchangeI, cache = TRUE, fig.cap="Close up of opposing processes within indiviuals (scale pr = 0 to pr = .1). Hidden Markov Model reveals that as people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from much greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.", include = TRUE, code_folding=TRUE}
plotI <-
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = conv_probability,
      ymin = conv_lower,
      ymax = conv_upper,
      colour = Group
    ),
    data = comboplot
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, stroke = 2) +
  geom_linerange(alpha = 1) +
  geom_smooth(se = F) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .96))  +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Annual Probability of Religious (Dis)affiliation w/ 95 CI: range: Pr = 0 to Pr = 1 ") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Religious (Dis)affiliation Dynamics in New Zealand: N = 53990,  years 2009--2021") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#d1495b", "#00798c")) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
plotI

plotIb <-
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = conv_probability,
      ymin = conv_lower,
      ymax = conv_upper,
      colour = Group
    ),
    data = comboplot
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, stroke = 2) +
  geom_linerange(alpha = 1) +
  geom_smooth(se = F) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .1))  +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Annual Probability of Religious (Dis)affiliation w/ 95 CI: range: Pr = 0 to Pr = .1 ") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Religious (Dis)affiliation Dynamics in New Zealand:  N = 53990, years 2009--2020") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#d1495b", "#00798c")) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

plotIb

plotI
# # to save file in your directory at correct resolution, uncomment below
#  file <- here::here("_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "figs", paste("aff_disaff", ".png", sep = ""))
# #
# ggsave(
#   file,
#   device = "png",
#   scale = 1,
#   width = 16,
#   height = 9,
#   units = "in",
#   dpi = 300,
#   limitsize = TRUE
# )
```

Appreciating that religious change is a Simpson's paradox
helps to build an intuition for why panel data are so
powerful. Sociologists have documented persistent declines
in religious affiliation across western societies, a process
Max Weber called "the disenchantment of the world"
[@Weber1946-zp]. This disenchantment theory is a central
dogma in the sociology of religion [@Norris2004-gu]. The
theory is accurate for describing society-level dynamics.
Indeed, NZAVS data confirm the society-level
"disenchantment." However, this central dogma is poorly
suited for understanding how religion and spirituality take
shape within the lives of individuals.

## Appendix

## Manifest State Change RELIGION

```{r}
m1raw  <- msm(
  Religious1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = dat,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
 # ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),  # remove emmissons matrix 
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE,
  # Not running without this assumption.
  gen.inits = TRUE
)


saveRDS(m1raw, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1raw.rds" ))

m1raw <- readRDS( here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1raw.rds" ))


## TRY ANOTHER 
datR <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1 & Wave !=2009) %>% # filtering only people who have responded in a year, no
  droplevels() %>%
  dplyr::select(Wave, Age, Wave, Religious1 , Id, Believe.God, Believe.Spirit, years, GenCohort, Male, Pol.Orient, Urban, Edu,
                Partner, Parent, AGREEABLENESS, CONSCIENTIOUSNESS, OPENNESS,HONESTY_HUMILITY,EXTRAVERSION,
                Religion.Church, NEUROTICISM,SmokerEver,EthnicCats) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(yearW  = as.numeric(Wave) - 1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% 
  filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  filter(n() != 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  mutate(Religious1 = as.numeric(Religious1))%>%
  mutate( cohort = as.numeric(GenCohort)-1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  add_tally(Religious1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Id) %>%
    filter(n > 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  select(-c(Wave, GenCohort, cohort, n)) %>%
  arrange( Id, yearW,  Religious1)
# make all numeric 
datR= as.data.frame(sapply(datR, as.numeric))

head(datR)
dim(datR)
# multi impute missing vales
library(Amelia)

# bounds for imp god belief 
bdsR <- matrix(c(2, 1, 2), nrow = 1, ncol = 3)
bdsR
head(datR)
datR = as.data.frame(datR)

datRimp <- amelia(
  set.seed=1234,
  datR, 
  cs= c("Id"),
  ts= c("yearW"),
  m = 1, # number of imputations
  ords = "Religious1",
  # noms = c(
  #   "EthnicCats_b","GenCohort"
  # ),
 # idvars=c("Id"), # yrs not working
  lags="Religious1",
  leads="Religious1",
  polytime = 2, # Allow polynomial
  intercs = F, # to many vars
  bounds = bdsR, 
) # ridge prior see: Amelia pdf documentation p.23

saveRDS(datRimp, here::here("_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox","mods", "datRimp"))


## DOES NOTE ESIMATE AGE CORRECTLY...
datir <-datRimp$imputations$imp1

str(datir)
min(datir$yearW, na.rm =TRUE)

## EILL METHOD

# make all numeric 
datR_fill<- datR%>% 
   tidyr::fill(Religious1, .direction = 'down') 

table(datR$Religious1)
table(datR_fill$Religious1)
table(datir$Religious1)



#nrow(df)

# unique ids
length(unique(datir$Id)) # 53790

table1::table1( ~Age|yearW, data = datR)
```

### HIDDEN MARKOV RELIGIOUS AMELIA

```{r  msm2, cache = TRUE, eval = FALSE}
### MSM ANALYIS BELOW ###
# commenting out model to save time (which I've saved in a file, below)
# initial matrix for transitions
library("msm")

Q0 <- rbind(c(.9, .1),
            c(.1, .9))

# intensity matrix
Q0

# Initialise
crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(Religious1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = datir,
                      qmatrix = Q0)

# model
m1_imp <- msm(
  Religious1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = datir,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
  ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE,
  # Not running without this assumption.
  gen.inits = TRUE
)

m1_imp
saveRDS(m1_imp, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_impt.rds" ))


m1_imp
saveRDS(m1_imp, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_impt.rds" ))


## FILL

# Initialise
crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(Religious1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = datR_fill,
                      qmatrix = Q0)


m1_FILL <- msm(
  Religious1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = datR_fill,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
  ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE,
  # Not running without this assumption.
  gen.inits = TRUE
)

saveRDS(m1_FILL, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_FILL.rds" ))



### MANIFST IMP

crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(Religious1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = datir,
                      qmatrix = Q0)


m1_manifest_imp  <- msm(
  Religious1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = datir,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
 # ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),  # for manifest model 
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE,
  # Not running without this assumption.
  gen.inits = TRUE
)

saveRDS(m1_manifest_imp, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_manifest_imp.rds" ))

m1_manifest_imp <- readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_manifest_imp.rds" ))



### MANIFEST FILLL 


crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(Religious1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = datR_fill,
                      qmatrix = Q0)


m1_manifest_fill <- msm(
  Religious1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = datR_fill,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
 # ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),  # for manifest model 
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE,
  # Not running without this assumption.
  gen.inits = TRUE
)

saveRDS(m1_manifest_fill, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_manifest_fill.rds" ))

m1_manifest_fill<- readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_manifest_fill.rds" ))






## STANDARD
head(daj)


# BETTER METHOD
daj <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1 & Wave !=2009) %>% # filtering only people who have responded in a year, no
  droplevels() %>%
  dplyr::select(Wave, Age, Wave, Religious1 , Id, TSCORE) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% 
  mutate(days = (TSCORE - min(TSCORE))) %>% # ZERO within individuals
  mutate(yearS = (TSCORE - min(TSCORE))/365) %>% # ZERO within individuals
  mutate(months = (TSCORE - min(TSCORE))/12) %>% # ZERO within individuals
  filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  filter(n() != 1) %>%
  add_tally(Religious1) %>%
  filter(n > 1) %>%
  mutate(notna = sum(!is.na(Religious1))) %>%
  filter(notna > 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  mutate(id = as.character(Id)) %>%
  mutate(yearW = as.numeric(Wave)-1) %>%
  arrange(Id, yearW, Wave) #%>%
 # tidyr::fill(Religious1, .direction = 'down') 

length(unique(daj$Id)) # 53409

#sum(is.na(daj$Religious1))


daj%>%
  filter(Id == 31470 | Id == 31471)

# daj2<-  daj %>%
#   group_by(Id) %>%
#   mutate(double = when(yearS == yearS, 1, 0)) 


# no ids measured twice 
table(is.na(daj$Religious1))

# Initialise
crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(Religious1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = daj,
                      qmatrix = Q0)


m1_standard <- msm(
  Religious1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = daj,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
  ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE,
  # Not running without this assumption.
  gen.inits = TRUE
)
warnings()

saveRDS(m1_FILL, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_FILL.rds" ))

```

Transition probabilities (averaged)

```{r}
# read model, (saved to save time)
m1_imp <-readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_imp.rds" ))
m1_FILL <-readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_FILL.rds" ))
m1_manifest_imp <- readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_manifest_imp.rds" ))

m1_manifest_fill <- readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","m1_manifest_fill.rds" ))


### TO KEEP CODE CONSISTENT
m1 <- m1_imp


#probability matrix
dt<-  print( round( msm::pmatrix.msm( m1 ) , 2 ) )
# label cols, rows:
rownames(dt) = c("Not-religious time 0", "Religious time 0")
colnames(dt) = c("Not-religious t+1","Religious t+1")

#table
dt%>%
 kbl(caption = "Hidden Markov Model: inferred population average religious state change, years 2009-2020")%>%
  kable_material(c("hover"))
```

LaTeX table

```{r, eval = FALSE}
dt %>%
  kbl(booktabs = T,
      format = "latex",
      caption = "Hidden Markov Model: inferred population average religious state change, years 2009-2020")
  
```

We can compare this with the raw (un-modelled) state
transition, which overstates overall change.

LaTeX table

```{r, eval = FALSE}
out %>%
  kbl(booktabs = T,
      format = "latex",
      caption ="Raw sample religious state change: years 2009-2020")
  
```

```{r, eval=FALSE}
# latex
xtable(out)
```

```{r  hmmchange, fig.cap="Hidden Markov Model reveals religious change within people differs from the society wide-aggregate trend. As people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.", echo =FALSE}
# Graph
m1 <- m1_imp
m1 <- m1_FILL
m1 <- m1_manifest_imp
m1 <- m1_manifest_fill

m1
oz<- as.matrix( msm::pmatrix.msm( m1 ) )
oz <- round(oz, digits = 2)


stateNames <- c("Secular (.63)","Religious (.37)")
row.names(oz) <- stateNames; colnames(oz) <- stateNames
oz
## need to transpose or else arrows go in the wrong direction
toz <- t(oz) 

#library(pracma)
plotmat(toz,
       # pos = c(1,2), 
       # lwd = 1, 
       # box.lwd = 2, 
        cex.txt = 0.9, 
        box.size = 0.09, 
       # box.type = "circle", 
        box.prop = 0.5,
        arr.pos =  0.4, 
        shadow.size = 0.001,
        box.col = c("lightgreen","lightblue","orange"),
       # arr.length=.1,
       # arr.width=.1,
        self.cex = .4,
        self.shifty = -.01,
        self.shiftx = .12,
        main = "Hidden Markov Model reveals religious switching\nNew Zealand: N = 53990, years 2009-2021")

```

## Graph

```{r}


p20 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 20), ci = "normal")
p20.Convert <- p20$estimates[1, 2]
p20.Convert.L <- p20$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p20.Convert.U <- p20$U[1, 2]
con.20 <- c(20, p20.Convert, p20.Convert.L, p20.Convert.U)
## 30 year olds
p30 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 30), ci = "normal")
p30.Convert <- p30$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p30.Convert.L <- p30$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p30.Convert.U <- p30$U[1, 2]
#combine
con.30 <- c(30, p30.Convert, p30.Convert.L, p30.Convert.U)
## 40 year old
p40 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 40), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p40.Convert <- p40$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p40.Convert.L <- p40$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p40.Convert.U <- p40$U[1, 2]
con.40 <- c(40, p40.Convert, p40.Convert.L, p40.Convert.U)
## 50 year old
p50 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 50), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p50.Convert <- p50$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p50.Convert.L <- p50$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p50.Convert.U <- p50$U[1, 2]
con.50 <- c(50, p50.Convert, p50.Convert.L, p50.Convert.U)
## 60 year olds
p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p60.Convert <- p60$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p60.Convert.L <- p60$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p60.Convert.U <- p60$U[1, 2]
con.60 <- c(60, p60.Convert, p60.Convert.L, p60.Convert.U)
## 70 year olds
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p70.Convert <- p70$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p70.Convert.L <- p70$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p70.Convert.U <- p70$U[1, 2]
con.70 <- c(70, p70.Convert, p70.Convert.L, p70.Convert.U)
### ## 80 year olds
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p80.Convert <- p80$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p80.Convert.L <- p80$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p80.Convert.U <- p80$U[1, 2]
con.80 <- c(80, p80.Convert, p80.Convert.L, p80.Convert.U)
# ### ## 90 year olds
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
# # Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p90.Convert <- p90$estimates[1, 2]
# # set lower bound
p90.Convert.L <- p90$L[1, 2]
p90.Convert.U <- p90$U[1, 2]
con.90 <- c(90, p90.Convert, p90.Convert.L, p90.Convert.U)
# ### create data frame for conversion plot
conplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(con.20, con.30, con.40, con.50, con.60, con.70, con.80, con.90))
colnames(conplot) <-
  c("age", "conv_probability", "conv_lower", "conv_upper")
library(ggplot2)
#library(wesanderson)
#wes_palette("Zissou1", type = c("discrete"))
#library("ggsci")



##### CREAT PLOT FOR DECONVERSION
## 20 year olds
p20 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 20), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p20.decon <- p20$estimates[2, 1]
p20.decon.L <- p20$L[2, 1]
p20.decon.U <- p20$U[2, 1]
decon.20 <- c(20, p20.decon, p20.decon.L, p20.decon.U)
p30 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 30), ci = "normal")
p30.decon <- p30$estimates[2, 1]
p30.decon.L <- p30$L[2, 1]
p30.decon.U <- p30$U[2, 1]
decon.30 <- c(30, p30.decon, p30.decon.L, p30.decon.U)
p40 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 40), ci = "normal")
p40.decon <- p40$estimates[2, 1]
p40.decon.L <- p40$L[2, 1]
p40.decon.U <- p40$U[2, 1]
decon.40 <- c(40, p40.decon, p40.decon.L, p40.decon.U)
p50 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 50), ci = "normal")
p50.decon <- p50$estimates[2, 1]
p50.decon.L <- p50$L[2, 1]
p50.decon.U <- p50$U[2, 1]
decon.50 <- c(50, p50.decon, p50.decon.L, p50.decon.U)
p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
p60.decon <- p60$estimates[2, 1]
p60.decon.L <- p60$L[2, 1]
p60.decon.U <- p60$U[2, 1]
decon.60 <- c(60, p60.decon, p60.decon.L, p60.decon.U)
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
p70.decon <- p70$estimates[2, 1]
p70.decon.L <- p70$L[2, 1]
p70.decon.U <- p70$U[2, 1]
decon.70 <- c(70, p70.decon, p70.decon.L, p70.decon.U)
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
p80.decon <- p80$estimates[2, 1]
p80.decon.L <- p80$L[2, 1]
p80.decon.U <- p80$U[2, 1]
decon.80 <- c(80, p80.decon, p80.decon.L, p80.decon.U)
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
p90.decon <- p90$estimates[2, 1]
p90.decon.L <- p90$L[2, 1]
p90.decon.U <- p90$U[2, 1]
decon.90 <- c(90, p90.decon, p90.decon.L, p90.decon.U)

### create data frame for conversion plot
deconplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(
    decon.20,
    decon.30,
    decon.40,
    decon.50,
    decon.60,
    decon.70,
    decon.80,
    decon.90
  ))
colnames(deconplot) <-
  c("age", "decon_probability", "decon_lower", "decon_upper")
library(ggplot2)
n = "bottom")

plot.decon
#grid.arrange(plot.con,plot.decon, ncol=2)
### SUPERIMPOSE
deconplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Disaffiliation", nrow(deconplot)))
conplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Affiliation", nrow(conplot)))
deconplot2 <- deconplot %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    conv_probability = decon_probability,
    conv_lower = decon_lower,
    conv_upper = decon_upper
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-c(decon_probability, decon_upper, decon_lower))

comboplot <- rbind(conplot, deconplot2)

library(ggsci )

plot_all_waves_collider_r_30 <-
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = conv_probability,
      ymin = conv_lower,
      ymax = conv_upper,
      colour = Group
    ),
    data = comboplot
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, stroke = 2) +
  geom_linerange(alpha = 1) +
  geom_smooth(se = F) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .30))  +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Annual Probability of Religious (Dis)Affiliation w/ 95 CI ") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Hidden Markov Model: (Dis)Afilliation Dynamics in New Zealand",
  subtitle= "N = 53790, years 2010-2021") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_color_aaas() + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

plot_all_waves_collider_r_12<-
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = conv_probability,
      ymin = conv_lower,
      ymax = conv_upper,
      colour = Group
    ),
    data = comboplot
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, stroke = 2) +
  geom_linerange(alpha = 1) +
  geom_smooth(se = F) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .12))  +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Annual Probability of Religious (Dis)Affiliation w/ 95 CI ") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Hidden Markov Model: (Dis)Afilliation Dynamics in New Zealand",
  subtitle= "N = 53790, years 2010-2021") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_color_aaas() + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

plotindividual_REL_30_imp <- readRDS(here::here(here::here("_posts", "religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods", "plotindividual_REL_30_imp")))
plotindividual_REL_05_imp <- readRDS(here::here(here::here("_posts", "religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods", "plotindividual_REL_05_imp")))

  
## FILL PLOTS 

plotindividual_REL_30_FILL <-
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = conv_probability,
      ymin = conv_lower,
      ymax = conv_upper,
      colour = Group
    ),
    data = comboplot
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, stroke = 2) +
  geom_linerange(alpha = 1) +
  geom_smooth(se = F) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .3))  +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Annual Probability of Religious (Dis)Affiliation w/ 95 CI ") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Hidden Markov Model: (Dis)Afilliation Dynamics in New Zealand",
  subtitle= "N = 53790, years 2010-2021") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_color_aaas() + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")


plotindividual_REL_30_FILL

  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = conv_probability,

  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, stroke = 2) +
  geom_linerange(alpha = 1) +
  geom_smooth(se = F) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .06))  +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Annual Probability of Religious (Dis)Affiliation w/ 95 CI ") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Hidden Markov Model: (Dis)Afilliation Dynamics in New Zealand",
  subtitle= "N = 53790, years 2010-2021") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +

## Data Trajectories

```{r}
library(LMest)
relwidelong <-  df %>%
  filter(Wave != 2009) %>% 
  dplyr::mutate(Religious = Religious1 - 1) %>%
  dplyr::select(Id, Religious, Wave)

##THIS HANGS
#out <- with(relwidelong, long2matrices(id = Id, X = Age, Y = Religious))

relwide <- spread(relwidelong, Wave, Religious)
head(relwide)

#relwide<-relwide[complete.cases(relwide), ]

Relidwide <-  df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Religious)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Id, Religious, Wave) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 11) %>%
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  spread(Wave, Religious)

Relidwide <- Relidwide[complete.cases(Relidwide),]
dim(Relidwide)

x_var_list <- names(Relidwide[, 2:13])

Relidwide

library(lcsm)


plot_trajectories(
  data = Relidwide,
  id_var = "Id",
  var_list = x_var_list,
  line_colour = "red",
  xlab = "Years",
  ylab = "Religious",
  connect_missing = F,
  scale_x_num = T,
  scale_x_num_start = 1,
  random_sample_frac = 0.049,
  seed = 1234
 # title_n = T
)  +
  facet_wrap( ~ Id)

# saved as 00rel_traj 16 X 9

df$Believe.God


godwide <-  df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Wave !=2009) %>%
 # dplyr::filter(!is.na(Religious)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Id, Believe.God, Wave) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 10) %>%
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  spread(Wave, Believe.God)

#godwide <- godwide[complete.cases(godwide),]
dim(godwide)

x_var_list <- names(Relidwide[, 2:12])

godwide

library(lcsm)


plot_trajectories(
  data = godwide,
  id_var = "Id",
  var_list = x_var_list,
  line_colour = "red",
  xlab = "Years",
  ylab = "Believe God",
  connect_missing = T,
  scale_x_num = T,
  scale_x_num_start = 1,
  random_sample_frac = 0.049,
  seed = 1234
 # title_n = T
)  +
  facet_wrap( ~ Id)


## Saved as 00god_traj 16 X 9

spiritwide <-  df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Wave !=2009) %>%
 # dplyr::filter(!is.na(Religious)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Id, Believe.Spirit, Wave) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 10) %>%
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  spread(Wave, Believe.Spirit)

#godwide <- godwide[complete.cases(godwide),]
dim(spiritwide)

x_var_list <- names(spiritwide[, 2:12])

spiritwide

library(lcsm)


plot_trajectories(
  data = spiritwide,
  id_var = "Id",
  var_list = x_var_list,
  line_colour = "red",
  xlab = "Years",
  ylab = "Believe Spirit",
  connect_missing = T,
  scale_x_num = T,
  scale_x_num_start = 1,
  random_sample_frac = 0.049,
  seed = 1234
 # title_n = T
)  +

### Graph of states

```{r}
# Graph
oz_20<- as.matrix( msm::pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 20)))
oz_20 <- round(oz_20, digits = 3)


stateNames <- c("Secular ","Religious")
row.names(oz_20) <- stateNames; colnames(oz_20) <- stateNames
oz_20
## need to transpose or else arrows go in the wrong direction
toz_20 <- t(oz_20) 

#library(pracma)
plotmat(toz_20,
       # pos = c(1,2), 
       # lwd = 1, 
       # box.lwd = 2, 
        cex.txt = 0.9, 
        box.size = 0.09, 
       # box.type = "circle", 
        box.prop = 0.5,
        arr.pos =  0.4, 
        shadow.size = 0.001,
        box.col = c("lightgreen","lightblue","orange"),
       # arr.length=.1,
       # arr.width=.1,
        self.cex = .4,
        self.shifty = -.01,
        self.shiftx = .12,
        main = "Hidden Markov Model reveals religious switching
       New Zealand: years 2009-2021, N = 53990, Age = 20")



# Graph
oz_70<- as.matrix( msm::pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 70)))
oz_70 <- round(oz_70, digits = 3)


stateNames <- c("Secular ","Religious")
row.names(oz_70) <- stateNames; colnames(oz_70) <- stateNames
oz_70
## need to transpose or else arrows go in the wrong direction
toz_70 <- t(oz_70) 

#library(pracma)
dev.off()
par(mfrow=c(1,2))

plotmat(toz_20,
       # pos = c(1,2), 
       # lwd = 1, 
       # box.lwd = 2, 
        cex.txt = 0.9, 
        box.size = 0.09, 
       # box.type = "circle", 
        box.prop = 0.5,
        arr.pos =  0.4, 
        shadow.size = 0.001,
        box.col = c("lightgreen","lightblue","orange"),
       # arr.length=.1,
       # arr.width=.1,
        self.cex = .4,
        self.shifty = -.01,
        self.shiftx = .12,
        main = "Hidden Markov Model: years 2009-2021, N = 53990, Age = 20")


plotmat(toz_70,
       # pos = c(1,2), 
       # lwd = 1, 
       # box.lwd = 2, 
        cex.txt = 0.9, 
        box.size = 0.09, 
       # box.type = "circle", 
        box.prop = 0.5,
        arr.pos =  0.4, 
        shadow.size = 0.001,
        box.col = c("lightgreen","lightblue","orange"),
       # arr.length=.1,
       # arr.width=.1,
        self.cex = .4,
        self.shifty = -.01,
        self.shiftx = .12,
        main = "Hidden Markov Model: years 2009-2021, N= 53990, Age = 70")

## Believe in GOD

#create dataframe

```{r}

df$Believe.God

names(df)
datG  <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1 & Wave !=2009) %>% # filtering only people who have responded in a year, no
  droplevels() %>%
  dplyr::select(Wave, Age, Wave, Religious1 , Id, Believe.God, years, GenCohort, Male, Pol.Orient, Urban, Edu,
                Partner, Parent, AGREEABLENESS, CONSCIENTIOUSNESS, OPENNESS,HONESTY_HUMILITY,EXTRAVERSION,
                Religion.Church, NEUROTICISM,SmokerEver,EthnicCats) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(yearW  = as.numeric(Wave) - 1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  filter(n() != 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  mutate(bg1 = as.numeric(Believe.God))%>%
  mutate( cohort = as.numeric(GenCohort)-1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  add_tally(bg1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Id) %>%
    filter(n > 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  select(-c(Wave, GenCohort, cohort, n,Believe.God)) %>%
  arrange( yearW, Id, bg1)
# make all numeric 
datG = as.data.frame(sapply(datG, as.numeric))

head(datG)
dim(datG)
# multi impute missing vales
library(Amelia)

# bounds for imp god belief 
bds <- matrix(c(21, 1, 2), nrow = 1, ncol = 3)
bds
head(datG)
datG = as.data.frame(datG)

datGimp <- amelia(
  set.seed=1234,
  datG, 
  cs= c("Id"),
  ts= c("yearW"),
  m = 1, # number of imputations
  ords = "bg1",
  # noms = c(
  #   "EthnicCats_b","GenCohort"
  # ),
 # idvars=c("Id"), # yrs not working
  lags="bg1",
  leads="bg1",
  polytime = 2, # Allow polynomial
  intercs = F, # to many vars
  bounds = bds, # lower upper bounds to Mus Prej
  empri = .05*nrow(datG)
) # ridge prior see: Amelia pdf documentation p.23

saveRDS(datGimp, here::here("_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox","mods", "datGimp"))

datig <-datGimp$imputations$imp1

str(datig)
min(datig$yearW, na.rm =TRUE)


#nrow(df)

# unique ids
length(unique(datig$Id)) #53388

table1::table1( ~bg1|yearW, data = datig)



## FILL METHOD
 
datG_FILL <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1 & Wave !=2009) %>% # filtering only people who have responded in a year, no
  droplevels() %>%
  dplyr::select(Wave, Age, Wave, Religious1 , Id, Believe.God, years, GenCohort, Male, Pol.Orient, Urban, Edu,
                Partner, Parent, AGREEABLENESS, CONSCIENTIOUSNESS, OPENNESS,HONESTY_HUMILITY,EXTRAVERSION,
                Religion.Church, NEUROTICISM,SmokerEver,EthnicCats) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(yearW  = as.numeric(Wave) - 1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  filter(n() != 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  mutate(bg1 = as.numeric(Believe.God))%>%
  mutate( cohort = as.numeric(GenCohort)-1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  add_tally(bg1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Id) %>%
    filter(n > 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  select(-c(Wave, GenCohort, cohort, n,Believe.God)) %>%
  arrange( Id, yearW, bg1) %>%
   tidyr::fill(bg1, .direction = 'down') # conservative 
# make all numeric 

datG_FILL

### MARKOV METHOD 

datG <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1 & Wave !=2009) %>% # filtering only people who have responded in a year, no
  droplevels() %>%
  dplyr::select(Wave, Age, Wave, Religious1 , Id, Believe.God, years, GenCohort, Male, Pol.Orient, Urban, Edu,
                Partner, Parent, AGREEABLENESS, CONSCIENTIOUSNESS, OPENNESS,HONESTY_HUMILITY,EXTRAVERSION,
                Religion.Church, NEUROTICISM,SmokerEver,EthnicCats) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(yearW  = as.numeric(Wave) - 1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  filter(n() != 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  mutate(bg1 = as.numeric(Believe.God))%>%
  mutate( cohort = as.numeric(GenCohort)-1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  add_tally(bg1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Id) %>%
    filter(n > 2) %>%  # at least two beliefs 
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  select(-c(Wave, GenCohort, cohort, Believe.God)) %>%
  arrange( Id, yearW, bg1)
# make all numeric 
datG_3 = as.data.frame(sapply(datG, as.numeric))

head(datG)
datG_3%>%
  select(Id, n) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>%
  arrange((n))

```

MSM Analysis

```{r  msm2, cache = TRUE, eval = FALSE}
### MSM ANALYIS BELOW ###
# commenting out model to save time (which I've saved in a file, below)
# initial matrix for transitions
library("msm")

Q0 <- rbind(c(.9, .1),
            c(.1, .9))

# intensity matrix
Q0

head(datig)

datig <- datig%>%
  arrange(Id, yearW)

# Initialise
crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(bg1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = datig,
                      qmatrix = Q0)
mg  <- msm(
  bg1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = datig,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
  gen.inits=TRUE,
  ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE
  # Not running without this assumption.
 # gen.inits = TRUE
)
mg

print( round( msm::pmatrix.msm( mg ) , 3 ) )


saveRDS(mg, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","mg.rds" ))


## MG FILL 


# Initialise
crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(bg1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = datG_FILL,
                      qmatrix = Q0)
mg_fill  <- msm(
  bg1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = datig,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
  gen.inits=TRUE,
  ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE
  # Not running without this assumption.
 # gen.inits = TRUE
)
mg

print( round( msm::pmatrix.msm( mg_fill ) , 3 ) )
print( round( msm::pmatrix.msm( mg ) , 3 ) )  ## NO DIFFERENCE. 


saveRDS(mg_fill, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","mg_fill.rds" ))


## ORDINARY MARKOV with THREE


Q0 <- rbind(c(.8, .2),
            c(.8, .2))

# intensity matrix
Q0

head(datig)


crudeinR
# Initialise
crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(bg1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = datG_3,
                      qmatrix = Q0)

# 
# # Wont run
# mg_3  <- msm(
#   bg1 ~ yearW,
#   Id,
#   data = datG_3,
#   covariates =  ~ Age,
#   qmatrix = crudeinR,
#   gen.inits=TRUE,
#   ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),
#   est.initprobs = TRUE,
#   exacttimes = TRUE
#   # Not running without this assumption.
#  # gen.inits = TRUE
# )
# warnings()



```

Transition probabilities (averaged)

```{r}
# read model, (saved to save time)

# note change of model name 
# USE IMPUTED
mg <-readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","mg" ))


mg_fill <-readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","mg_fill.rds" ))


m1 <- mg
#probability matrix
dt<-  print( round( msm::pmatrix.msm( m1 ) , 3 ) )

# label cols, rows:
rownames(dt) = c("Not-Believe God time 0", "Believe God time 0")
colnames(dt) = c("Not-Believe God t+1","Believe God t+1")

#table
dt%>%
 kbl(caption = "Hidden Markov Model: inferred population average Belief in God state change, years 2009-2021")%>%
  kable_material(c("hover"))
```

LaTeX table

```{r, eval = FALSE}
dt %>%
  kbl(booktabs = T,
      format = "latex",
      caption = "Hidden Markov Model: inferred population average belief in God state change, years 2009-2021")
  
```

```{r  hmmchange, fig.cap="Hidden Markov Model reveals religious change within people differs from the society wide-aggregate trend. As people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.", echo =FALSE}
# Graph
oz<- as.matrix( msm::pmatrix.msm( m1 ) )
oz <- round(oz, digits = 3)
m1
oz
stateNames <- c("No Belief God (.46)","Belief God (.54)")
row.names(oz) <- stateNames; colnames(oz) <- stateNames
oz
## need to transpose or else arrows go in the wrong direction
toz <- t(oz) 

#library(pracma)
plotmat(toz,
       # pos = c(1,2), 
       # lwd = 1, 
       # box.lwd = 2, 
        cex.txt = 0.9, 
        box.size = 0.09, 
       # box.type = "circle", 
        box.prop = 0.5,
        arr.pos =  0.4, 
        shadow.size = 0.001,
        box.col = c("lightgreen","lightblue","orange"),
       # arr.length=.1,
       # arr.width=.1,
        self.cex = .4,
        self.shifty = -.01,
        self.shiftx = .12,
        main = "Hidden Markov Model reveals low transition to Belief in God \nNew Zealand: N = 53388, years 2010-2021")

# god_markov 12 x 9 
```

## Graph

```{r  hmmchange2, cache = TRUE, fig.cap="Hidden Markov Model reveals religious change within people differs from the society wide-aggregate trend. As people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from much greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.", include = TRUE, code_folding=TRUE}

# note to self, this code can be improved
### CREATE PLOT  ###  CONVERSION AGE PLOT
## 20 year olds

#m1 <-readRDS(here::here( "models","mnm-simpson-paradox-religious-change" ))

p20 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 20), ci = "normal")
p20.Convert <- p20$estimates[1, 2]
p20.Convert.L <- p20$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p20.Convert.U <- p20$U[1, 2]
con.20 <- c(20, p20.Convert, p20.Convert.L, p20.Convert.U)
## 30 year olds
p30 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 30), ci = "normal")
p30.Convert <- p30$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p30.Convert.L <- p30$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p30.Convert.U <- p30$U[1, 2]
#combine
con.30 <- c(30, p30.Convert, p30.Convert.L, p30.Convert.U)
## 40 year old
p40 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 40), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p40.Convert <- p40$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p40.Convert.L <- p40$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p40.Convert.U <- p40$U[1, 2]
con.40 <- c(40, p40.Convert, p40.Convert.L, p40.Convert.U)
## 50 year old
p50 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 50), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p50.Convert <- p50$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p50.Convert.L <- p50$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p50.Convert.U <- p50$U[1, 2]
con.50 <- c(50, p50.Convert, p50.Convert.L, p50.Convert.U)
## 60 year olds
p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p60.Convert <- p60$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p60.Convert.L <- p60$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p60.Convert.U <- p60$U[1, 2]
con.60 <- c(60, p60.Convert, p60.Convert.L, p60.Convert.U)
## 70 year olds
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p70.Convert <- p70$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p70.Convert.L <- p70$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p70.Convert.U <- p70$U[1, 2]
con.70 <- c(70, p70.Convert, p70.Convert.L, p70.Convert.U)
### ## 80 year olds
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p80.Convert <- p80$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p80.Convert.L <- p80$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p80.Convert.U <- p80$U[1, 2]
con.80 <- c(80, p80.Convert, p80.Convert.L, p80.Convert.U)
# ### ## 90 year olds
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
# # Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p90.Convert <- p90$estimates[1, 2]
# # set lower bound
p90.Convert.L <- p90$L[1, 2]
p90.Convert.U <- p90$U[1, 2]
con.90 <- c(90, p90.Convert, p90.Convert.L, p90.Convert.U)
# ### create data frame for conversion plot
conplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(con.20, con.30, con.40, con.50, con.60, con.70, con.80, con.90))
colnames(conplot) <-
  c("age", "conv_probability", "conv_lower", "conv_upper")
library(ggplot2)
#library(wesanderson)
#wes_palette("Zissou1", type = c("discrete"))
#library("ggsci")

plot.con <-
  ggplot(aes(
    x = age,
    y = conv_probability,
    ymin = conv_lower,
    ymax = conv_upper
  ),
  data = conplot) + geom_point(stroke = 2, colour = "#d1495b")  +
  geom_linerange(alpha =   1, colour = "#d1495b") +
  geom_smooth(colour = "#d1495b",
              method = "loess",
              se = FALSE)  +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .1)) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Belief in God Transition Probability w/ 95% CI") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Religious (Dis)Belief in God Dynamics in New Zealand: years 2010-2021")
#plot.con
#plot.con
#m1

##### CREAT PLOT FOR DECONVERSION
## 20 year olds
p20 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 20), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p20.decon <- p20$estimates[2, 1]
p20.decon.L <- p20$L[2, 1]
p20.decon.U <- p20$U[2, 1]
decon.20 <- c(20, p20.decon, p20.decon.L, p20.decon.U)
p30 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 30), ci = "normal")
p30.decon <- p30$estimates[2, 1]
p30.decon.L <- p30$L[2, 1]
p30.decon.U <- p30$U[2, 1]
decon.30 <- c(30, p30.decon, p30.decon.L, p30.decon.U)
p40 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 40), ci = "normal")
p40.decon <- p40$estimates[2, 1]
p40.decon.L <- p40$L[2, 1]
p40.decon.U <- p40$U[2, 1]
decon.40 <- c(40, p40.decon, p40.decon.L, p40.decon.U)
p50 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 50), ci = "normal")
p50.decon <- p50$estimates[2, 1]
p50.decon.L <- p50$L[2, 1]
p50.decon.U <- p50$U[2, 1]
decon.50 <- c(50, p50.decon, p50.decon.L, p50.decon.U)
p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
p60.decon <- p60$estimates[2, 1]
p60.decon.L <- p60$L[2, 1]
p60.decon.U <- p60$U[2, 1]
decon.60 <- c(60, p60.decon, p60.decon.L, p60.decon.U)
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
p70.decon <- p70$estimates[2, 1]
p70.decon.L <- p70$L[2, 1]
p70.decon.U <- p70$U[2, 1]
decon.70 <- c(70, p70.decon, p70.decon.L, p70.decon.U)
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
p80.decon <- p80$estimates[2, 1]
p80.decon.L <- p80$L[2, 1]
p80.decon.U <- p80$U[2, 1]
decon.80 <- c(80, p80.decon, p80.decon.L, p80.decon.U)
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
p90.decon <- p90$estimates[2, 1]
p90.decon.L <- p90$L[2, 1]
p90.decon.U <- p90$U[2, 1]
decon.90 <- c(90, p90.decon, p90.decon.L, p90.decon.U)

### create data frame for conversion plot
deconplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(
    decon.20,
    decon.30,
    decon.40,
    decon.50,
    decon.60,
    decon.70,
    decon.80,
    decon.90
  ))
colnames(deconplot) <-
  c("age", "decon_probability", "decon_lower", "decon_upper")
library(ggplot2)
plot.decon <-
  ggplot(aes(
    x = age,
    y = decon_probability,
    ymin = decon_lower,
    ymax = decon_upper
  ),
  data = deconplot) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1,
             colour = "#00798c",
             stroke = 2) +   geom_linerange(alpha = 1, colour = "#00798c") +
  #scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,.1))    +
  geom_smooth(colour = "#00798c", se = T) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Religious Disaffiliation Transition Probability w/ 95% CI") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Religious (Dis)affiliation Dynamics")  +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

#library(gridExtra)

#grid.arrange(plot.con,plot.decon, ncol=2)
### SUPERIMPOSE
deconplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Dis-Belief", nrow(deconplot)))
conplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Belief", nrow(conplot)))
deconplot2 <- deconplot %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    conv_probability = decon_probability,
    conv_lower = decon_lower,
    conv_upper = decon_upper
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-c(decon_probability, decon_upper, decon_lower))

comboplot <- rbind(conplot, deconplot2)
#comboplot
# get initial probabilities for title label
#pmatrix.msm(m1)

library(ggsci )
plotindividual_GOD_30_imp <-
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = conv_probability,
      ymin = conv_lower,
      ymax = conv_upper,
      colour = Group
    ),
    data = comboplot
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, stroke = 2) +

  labs(x = "Age", y = "Annual Probability of Religious (Dis)Belief w/ 95 CI ") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Hidden Markov Model: (Dis)Belief in God Dynamics in New Zealand",
  subtitle= "N = 53990, years 2010-2021") +

  scale_color_npg() +

plotindividual_GOD_05_imp <-

  subtitle= "N = 53990, years 2010-2021") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_color_npg() + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

## Belief God State Change

## Data Trajectories

```{r}
library(LMest)


relwidelong <-  df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(bg = Believe.God) %>%
  dplyr::select(Id, bg, Wave)

##THIS HANGS
#out <- with(relwidelong, long2matrices(id = Id, X = Age, Y = Religious))

relwide <- spread(relwidelong, Wave, bg)
head(relwide)

#relwide<-relwide[complete.cases(relwide), ]
Relidwide

Relidwide <-  df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1 & Wave != 2009) %>%
 # dplyr::filter(!is.na(Religious)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Id, Believe.God, Wave) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 8) %>%
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  spread(Wave, Believe.God)

#Relidwide <- Relidwide[complete.cases(Relidwide),]
dim(Relidwide)

x_var_list <- names(Relidwide[, 2:12])

Relidwide


plot_trajectories(
  data = Relidwide,
  id_var = "Id",
  var_list = x_var_list,
  line_colour = "red",
  xlab = "Years",
  ylab = "Belief God",
  connect_missing = T,
  scale_x_num = T,
  scale_x_num_start = 1,
  random_sample_frac = 0.011,
  seed = 1234
 # title_n = T
)  +
  facet_wrap( ~ Id)

#god_trajectories 16 X9 

```

MSM Analysis BELIEF MARKOV MANIFEST HIDDEN

```{r  msm2, cache = TRUE, eval = FALSE}
### MSM ANALYIS BELOW ###
# commenting out model to save time (which I've saved in a file, below)
# initial matrix for transitions
library("msm")

Q0 <- rbind(c(.9, .1),
            c(.1, .9))

# intensity matrix
Q0

head(datig)

datig <- datig%>%
  arrange(Id, yearW)

# Initialise
crudeinR <-
  msm::crudeinits.msm(bg1 ~ yearW, Id, # Id = identifier
                      data = datig,
                      qmatrix = Q0)

mg_nothidden  <- msm(
  bg1 ~ yearW,
  Id,
  data = datig,
  covariates =  ~ Age,
  qmatrix = crudeinR,
  gen.inits=TRUE,
 # ematrix = rbind(c(.1, .1), c(.1, .1)),
  est.initprobs = TRUE,
  exacttimes = TRUE
  # Not running without this assumption.
 # gen.inits = TRUE
)
mg_nothidden

print( round( msm::pmatrix.msm( mg_nothidden ) , 3 ) )


saveRDS(mg_nothidden, here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","mg_nothidden.rds" ))

```

Transition probabilities (averaged)

```{r}
# read model, (saved to save time)

# note change of model name 
mg_nothidden <-readRDS(here::here( "_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox", "mods","mg_nothidden.rds" ))
m1 <- mg_nothidden
#probability matrix
dt<-  print( round( msm::pmatrix.msm( m1 ) , 3 ) )

# label cols, rows:
rownames(dt) = c("Not-Believe God time 0", "Believe God time 0")
colnames(dt) = c("Not-Believe God t+1","Believe God t+1")

#table
dt%>%
 kbl(caption = "State Change Markov Model: inferred population average Belief in God state change, years 2009-2021")%>%
  kable_material(c("hover"))
```

LaTeX table

```{r, eval = FALSE}
dt %>%
  kbl(booktabs = T,
      format = "latex",
      caption = "State Change Markov Model: inferred population average belief in God state change, years 2009-2021")
```

```{r  hmmchange, fig.cap="Hidden Markov Model reveals religious change within people differs from the society wide-aggregate trend. As people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.", echo =FALSE}

# Graph
oz<- as.matrix( msm::pmatrix.msm( m1 ) )
oz <- round(oz, digits = 3)
m1
oz
stateNames <- c("No Belief God (.46)","Belief God (.54)")
row.names(oz) <- stateNames; colnames(oz) <- stateNames
oz
## need to transpose or else arrows go in the wrong direction
toz <- t(oz) 

#library(pracma)
plotmat(toz,
       # pos = c(1,2), 
       # lwd = 1, 
       # box.lwd = 2, 
        cex.txt = 0.9, 
        box.size = 0.09, 
       # box.type = "circle", 
        box.prop = 0.5,
        arr.pos =  0.4, 
        shadow.size = 0.001,
        box.col = c("lightgreen","lightblue","orange"),
       # arr.length=.1,
       # arr.width=.1,
        self.cex = .4,
        self.shifty = -.01,
        self.shiftx = .12,
        main = "State Markov Model reveals low transition to Belief in God \nNew Zealand: N = 53388, years 2010-2021")

# god_markov 12 x 9 
```

## Graph

```{r  hmmchange2, cache = TRUE, fig.cap="Hidden Markov Model reveals religious change within people differs from the society wide-aggregate trend. As people age, they are more likely to affiliate and less likely to disaffiliate. The population gap comes from much greater disaffiliation among young people than affiliation.", include = TRUE, code_folding=TRUE}

# note to self, this code can be improved
### CREATE PLOT  ###  CONVERSION AGE PLOT
## 20 year olds

#m1 <-readRDS(here::here( "models","mnm-simpson-paradox-religious-change" ))

p20 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 20), ci = "normal")
p20.Convert <- p20$estimates[1, 2]
p20.Convert.L <- p20$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p20.Convert.U <- p20$U[1, 2]
con.20 <- c(20, p20.Convert, p20.Convert.L, p20.Convert.U)
## 30 year olds
p30 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 30), ci = "normal")
p30.Convert <- p30$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p30.Convert.L <- p30$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p30.Convert.U <- p30$U[1, 2]
#combine
con.30 <- c(30, p30.Convert, p30.Convert.L, p30.Convert.U)
## 40 year old
p40 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 40), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p40.Convert <- p40$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p40.Convert.L <- p40$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p40.Convert.U <- p40$U[1, 2]
con.40 <- c(40, p40.Convert, p40.Convert.L, p40.Convert.U)
## 50 year old
p50 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 50), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p50.Convert <- p50$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p50.Convert.L <- p50$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p50.Convert.U <- p50$U[1, 2]
con.50 <- c(50, p50.Convert, p50.Convert.L, p50.Convert.U)
## 60 year olds
p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p60.Convert <- p60$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p60.Convert.L <- p60$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p60.Convert.U <- p60$U[1, 2]
con.60 <- c(60, p60.Convert, p60.Convert.L, p60.Convert.U)
## 70 year olds
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p70.Convert <- p70$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p70.Convert.L <- p70$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p70.Convert.U <- p70$U[1, 2]
con.70 <- c(70, p70.Convert, p70.Convert.L, p70.Convert.U)
### ## 80 year olds
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p80.Convert <- p80$estimates[1, 2]
# set lower bound
p80.Convert.L <- p80$L[1, 2]
# get upper bound
p80.Convert.U <- p80$U[1, 2]
con.80 <- c(80, p80.Convert, p80.Convert.L, p80.Convert.U)
# ### ## 90 year olds
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
# # Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p90.Convert <- p90$estimates[1, 2]
# # set lower bound
p90.Convert.L <- p90$L[1, 2]
p90.Convert.U <- p90$U[1, 2]
con.90 <- c(90, p90.Convert, p90.Convert.L, p90.Convert.U)
# ### create data frame for conversion plot
conplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(con.20, con.30, con.40, con.50, con.60, con.70, con.80, con.90))
colnames(conplot) <-
  c("age", "conv_probability", "conv_lower", "conv_upper")
library(ggplot2)
#library(wesanderson)
#wes_palette("Zissou1", type = c("discrete"))
#library("ggsci")

plot.con <-
  ggplot(aes(
    x = age,
    y = conv_probability,
    ymin = conv_lower,
    ymax = conv_upper
  ),
  data = conplot) + geom_point(stroke = 2, colour = "#d1495b")  +
  geom_linerange(alpha =   1, colour = "#d1495b") +
  geom_smooth(colour = "#d1495b",
              method = "loess",
              se = FALSE)  +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .1)) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Belief in God Transition Probability w/ 95% CI") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "(Dis)Belief in God Dynamics in New Zealand: years 2010-2021")
#plot.con
#plot.con
#m1

##### CREAT PLOT FOR DECONVERSION
## 20 year olds
p20 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 20), ci = "normal")
# Get Transition to Relid Estimate
p20.decon <- p20$estimates[2, 1]
p20.decon.L <- p20$L[2, 1]
p20.decon.U <- p20$U[2, 1]
decon.20 <- c(20, p20.decon, p20.decon.L, p20.decon.U)
p30 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 30), ci = "normal")
p30.decon <- p30$estimates[2, 1]
p30.decon.L <- p30$L[2, 1]
p30.decon.U <- p30$U[2, 1]
decon.30 <- c(30, p30.decon, p30.decon.L, p30.decon.U)
p40 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 40), ci = "normal")
p40.decon <- p40$estimates[2, 1]
p40.decon.L <- p40$L[2, 1]
p40.decon.U <- p40$U[2, 1]
decon.40 <- c(40, p40.decon, p40.decon.L, p40.decon.U)
p50 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 50), ci = "normal")
p50.decon <- p50$estimates[2, 1]
p50.decon.L <- p50$L[2, 1]
p50.decon.U <- p50$U[2, 1]
decon.50 <- c(50, p50.decon, p50.decon.L, p50.decon.U)
p60 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 60), ci = "normal")
p60.decon <- p60$estimates[2, 1]
p60.decon.L <- p60$L[2, 1]
p60.decon.U <- p60$U[2, 1]
decon.60 <- c(60, p60.decon, p60.decon.L, p60.decon.U)
p70 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 70), ci = "normal")
p70.decon <- p70$estimates[2, 1]
p70.decon.L <- p70$L[2, 1]
p70.decon.U <- p70$U[2, 1]
decon.70 <- c(70, p70.decon, p70.decon.L, p70.decon.U)
p80 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 80), ci = "normal")
p80.decon <- p80$estimates[2, 1]
p80.decon.L <- p80$L[2, 1]
p80.decon.U <- p80$U[2, 1]
decon.80 <- c(80, p80.decon, p80.decon.L, p80.decon.U)
p90 <-
  pmatrix.msm(m1, covariates = list(Age = 90), ci = "normal")
p90.decon <- p90$estimates[2, 1]
p90.decon.L <- p90$L[2, 1]
p90.decon.U <- p90$U[2, 1]
decon.90 <- c(90, p90.decon, p90.decon.L, p90.decon.U)

### create data frame for conversion plot
deconplot <-
  data.frame(rbind(
    decon.20,
    decon.30,
    decon.40,
    decon.50,
    decon.60,
    decon.70,
    decon.80,
    decon.90
  ))
colnames(deconplot) <-
  c("age", "decon_probability", "decon_lower", "decon_upper")
library(ggplot2)
plot.decon <-
  ggplot(aes(
    x = age,
    y = decon_probability,
    ymin = decon_lower,
    ymax = decon_upper
  ),
  data = deconplot) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1,
             colour = "#00798c",
             stroke = 2) +   geom_linerange(alpha = 1, colour = "#00798c") +
  #scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,.1))    +
  geom_smooth(colour = "#00798c", se = T) +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Religious Disaffiliation Transition Probability w/ 95% CI") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(title = " (Dis)affiliation Dynamics")  +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

#library(gridExtra)

#grid.arrange(plot.con,plot.decon, ncol=2)
### SUPERIMPOSE
deconplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Dis-Belief", nrow(deconplot)))
conplot$Group <- as.factor(rep("Belief", nrow(conplot)))
deconplot2 <- deconplot %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    conv_probability = decon_probability,
    conv_lower = decon_lower,
    conv_upper = decon_upper
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-c(decon_probability, decon_upper, decon_lower))

comboplot <- rbind(conplot, deconplot2)
#comboplot
# get initial probabilities for title label
#pmatrix.msm(m1)
comboplot
library(ggplot2)

# plot.combo <-
#   ggplot(

#       x = age,

#   #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
#   scale_colour_manual(values = c("#d1495b", "#00798c")) +
#   theme(legend.position = "bottom")
# plot.combo

library(ggsci )
plotindividual_GOD_STATE_CHANGE_30_imp <-
  ggplot(
    aes(
      x = age,
      y = conv_probability,
      ymin = conv_lower,
      ymax = conv_upper,
      colour = Group
    ),
    data = comboplot
  ) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, stroke = 2) +
  geom_linerange(alpha = 1) +
  geom_smooth(se = F) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, .3))  +
  labs(x = "Age", y = "Annual Probability of Religious (Dis)Belief w/ 95 CI ") + theme_bw() + labs(title = "Manifest Markove Model: (Dis)Belief in God Dynamics in New Zealand",
  subtitle= "N = 53990, years 2010-2021") +
  #scale_colour_manual(values=c( "#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
  scale_colour_npg() + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

plotindividual_GOD_STATE_CHANGE_30_imp

## Data Trajectories

```{r}
library(LMest)


relwidelong <-  df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(bg = Believe.God) %>%
  dplyr::select(Id, bg, Wave)

##THIS HANGS
#out <- with(relwidelong, long2matrices(id = Id, X = Age, Y = Religious))

relwide <- spread(relwidelong, Wave, bg)
head(relwide)

#relwide<-relwide[complete.cases(relwide), ]
Relidwide

Relidwide <-  df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1 & Wave != 2009) %>%
 # dplyr::filter(!is.na(Religious)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Id, Believe.God, Wave) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 8) %>%
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  spread(Wave, Believe.God)

#Relidwide <- Relidwide[complete.cases(Relidwide),]
dim(Relidwide)

x_var_list <- names(Relidwide[, 2:12])

Relidwide


plot_trajectories(
  data = Relidwide,
  id_var = "Id",
  var_list = x_var_list,
  line_colour = "red",
  xlab = "Years",
  ylab = "Belief God",
  connect_missing = T,
  scale_x_num = T,
  scale_x_num_start = 1,
  random_sample_frac = 0.011,
  seed = 1234
 # title_n = T
)  +
  facet_wrap( ~ Id)

#god_trajectories 16 X9 

```

### BELIEF IN SPIRIT

#create dataframe

```{r}
names(df)
datS  <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1 & Wave !=2009) %>% # filtering only people who have responded in a year, no
  droplevels() %>%
  dplyr::select(Wave, Age, Wave, Religious1 , Id, Believe.Spirit, years, GenCohort, Male, Pol.Orient, Urban, Edu,
                Partner, Parent, AGREEABLENESS, CONSCIENTIOUSNESS, OPENNESS,HONESTY_HUMILITY,EXTRAVERSION,
                Religion.Church, NEUROTICISM,SmokerEver,EthnicCats) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(yearW  = as.numeric(Wave) - 1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  filter(n() != 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  mutate(bs1 = as.numeric(Believe.Spirit))%>%
  mutate( cohort = as.numeric(GenCohort)-1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  add_tally(bs1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Id) %>%
    filter(n > 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  select(-c(Wave, GenCohort, cohort, n,Believe.Spirit)) %>%
  arrange( Id, yearW, bs1) %>%
  tidyr::fill(bs1, .direction = 'down') # conservative 

# make all numeric 
datS = as.data.frame(sapply(datS, as.numeric))

head(datS)
dim(datS)
# multi impute missing vales
library(Amelia)

# bounds for imp god belief 
bds <- matrix(c(21, 1, 2), nrow = 1, ncol = 3)
bds
head(datS)
datS = as.data.frame(datS)

datSimp <- amelia(
  set.seed=1234,
  datS, 
  cs= c("Id"),
  ts= c("yearW"),
  m = 1, # number of imputations
  ords = "bs1",
  # noms = c(
  #   "EthnicCats_b","GenCohort"
  # ),
 # idvars=c("Id"), # yrs not working
  lags="bs1",
  leads="bs1",
  polytime = 2, # Allow polynomial
  intercs = F, # to many vars
  bounds = bds, # lower upper bounds to Mus Prej
  empri = .05*nrow(datS)
) # ridge prior see: Amelia pdf documentation p.23

saveRDS(datSimp, here::here("_posts","religious_simpsons_paradox","mods", "datSimp"))

datis <-datSimp$imputations$imp1

str(datis)
min(datis$yearW, na.rm =TRUE)


## FILL DATA FRAME = BETTER 
datS_fill  <- df %>%
  dplyr::filter(YearMeasured == 1 & Wave !=2009) %>% # filtering only people who have responded in a year, no
  droplevels() %>%
  dplyr::select(Wave, Age, Wave, Religious1 , Id, Believe.Spirit, years, GenCohort, Male, Pol.Orient, Urban, Edu,
                Partner, Parent, AGREEABLENESS, CONSCIENTIOUSNESS, OPENNESS,HONESTY_HUMILITY,EXTRAVERSION,
                Religion.Church, NEUROTICISM,SmokerEver,EthnicCats) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(yearW  = as.numeric(Wave) - 1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  filter(n() != 0) %>%
  filter(n() != 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  mutate(bs1 = as.numeric(Believe.Spirit))%>%
  mutate( cohort = as.numeric(GenCohort)-1) %>%
  group_by(Id) %>% filter(n() > 1) %>% # have responded to at least 2 x waves
  add_tally(bs1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Id) %>%
    filter(n > 1) %>%
  ungroup(Id) %>%
  droplevels() %>%
  select(-c(Wave, GenCohort, cohort, n,Believe.Spirit)) %>%
  arrange( Id, yearW, bs1) %>%
  tidyr::fill(bs1, .direction = 'down') # conservative 


#nrow(df)

# unique ids
length(unique(datis$Id)) #53728

table1::table1( ~bs1|yearW, data = datis)

```
